transcript.text: Oh, that's right.
We might be pushing it to the limit.
Do you guys still have that router in the corner over there?
I think we've placed some hotspots around, but I'm one of the conference rooms, so I'm kind of like on the edge.
But anyways, pretty clear.
Arshad just came off.
He's going to come back in.
I was just, I told him, oh, there you go.
Hey, Kyle.
How are you doing?
Good.
How about yourself, Arshad?
I'm good.
I'm good.
So thanks a lot for setting up this time and joining the call.
I've got about 25 minutes, but we thought John's in a flight, so he won't be able to join.
He's our chief monitoring officer, but we wanted to just get a sense of what you guys do and how different are you from the platforms that we're using?
So, you know, it would be good to get a background, understand the clients you're working with, and if you've done any work in the B2B dev tooling space, and then we can go from there.
Yeah, happy to share a bit more, for sure, to clarify.
Bye.

Jump into a particular question, just a quick intro, I'll keep it brief since we're starting a little bit late here, but how to grow VP of customer growth, lead on the sales side, just about a month and a half or so ago, I leading the client services side of the business, just about a year ago, I signed up to be the first customer.
So my background is actually more in digital and growth.
I spent 19 years previous to this agency side in-house.
A lot of my experience lies in brands that want to diversify their channels, be less reliant on things like paid search.

A lot of times I'd be brought in and they were a bit too stuck in spending too much on paid and need to figure out how they could find other paid channels, and organic was usually a huge focus of how do you scale organic.
So a variety of different stops like Anaplan, Trey, Sendoso, and most recently a company called Homebase, where we had some really good growth.
You know, we were 170% up year over year in traffic and seen really, really strong signups on the organic.
But I was at a place where, you know, a lot of things were shifting fast in the market.
These were likely getting trimmed in the second half of last year.
My targets weren't going down at the same rate.
And so part of the journey that brought me here was that I met Marcel, our CEO and co-founder.
I'm not sure if you've seen some of his content.
That's how a lot of people find us.

He was doing different workshops and sharing how he was building Arian Gara workflows that enable his team to essentially, you know, our research, creation of content, just move really fast and putting out high quality content.
And I was at a phase where I was spending a lot on freelancers, my internal team, and it was just taking a lot of time and coordination.
And so it was really just the, you know, perfect time and place.
I was also building off some automated-based things I was doing, just, you know, try being an automation-based company.
And, yeah, you know, now we're just closed our Series A.
70 employees have been a while to ride.
A lot of those being in the B2B SaaS space, certainly a lot of those that are DevTool-focused, know, one of the things that we did early on that.

You know, it formed a lot of our approach and processes, and I will say transparently, was a little worried about this in the early days, was really taking on the challenge of technical content, because that's really informed a lot of what we've done.

If you're going to meet that bar, if there's going to be a particular audience who's going to read this, and if you say a certain thing the wrong way, they're not going to trust it, what is your approach to being able to create content at that speed that ensures you don't fall into that?
And so, I'm happy to share a bit more specifics on how we approach that, just wanted to know that, given that you shared a little bit of that up front.

So we have a variety of different ways through our process and our workflows that help us put our best foot forward in there and work closely with teams to kind of calibrate on what makes sense for them.
So that's a quick on me.
Arshad, you mentioned other platforms, maybe services, but can you just clarify?
Arshad, you were saying compare us to, can you just give me a little bit more context?
Arshad, you
What's on your mind there?

Yeah, I mean, if you're talking about the content space, we are actually doing a good amount of internal, we do a lot of internal content, and we're also using a third-party services provider who writes a lot of content for us.
So that's really what we're using.
And then we have a whole marketing stack that we use right across the board.
So are you guys focused on more content production?
Is that the way to think about it, or how do we think about it?

Yeah, you know, for us, essentially, you know, our aim is largely on the content side of how we can build an organic content engine for our customers, and that ultimately opens up a variety of different paths, typically.

So what content means and the form of that can be different things, but where we tend to start the vast majority of our relationships is focusing on what we call editorial content, longer-form content that can be blogged, but not limited to that.
And just the purpose of that, there's a lot of coordination that goes in.
In learning that goes into understanding voice styles, how to be on the brand consistently with you, who are the personas, how do we get feedback on what topics are resonating with them or not.

As we do that, that naturally can open up other paths for us to start to diversify what we do that can still be somewhat in the content space, but it's also how do you distribute and how do you convert people who are engaging with this content in a variety of ways.
But that could be things like helping our customers with things like newsletters.
We're doing that with Udemy, for example, and helping build their newsletter and developing essentially a publication around that.
That can be building things like lead magnets, helping them with social.
It's just that's not like we don't typically do those as one offs where we would start a relationship.
It is more, how do we lay that right foundation from a more holistic content strategy standpoint and focusing editorially first really helps there because ultimately.
We our customers to think and act more like publishers.

I'm not sure, you know, this is what you see, but what we are at least seeing is that's becoming much more common in the SaaS space and seeing a lot of value because when you think like publishers, typically you are thinking about your personas at a deeper level and how can you create content that is truly highly valuable for them.
Sometimes there is a stigma around, say, content that could drive SEO, that it is like purely for that and low quality.
That is in no way like how we like to operate and wouldn't even view ourselves as like a conventional SEO shop.

And so, but yes, to, you know, put a pin on answering your question there directly, content is the bread and butter of what we do, but with a lot of different things that we can do along the way to enhance that.
And so, you know, whole thing is essentially a combination of a team of experts, highly deep in growth and content and other aspects that are necessary to make that function work better.
Enhanced and enriched with AI-driven workflows.
We have a product side, platform side that we're building with our engineering team that enables this team of experts to move super, super fast.
We're also at a phase where, in the not-too-distant feature, we can start opening up this platform for our customers and them to be a bit hands-on.

But largely in our journey, our focus has been primarily providing that as a service because we've just found that a lot of customers in the early days, they were inspired, excited by all the things that we're doing.
Myself, going back to the workshop days, the reality was, like, don't have time to build the workflows, especially continue to maintain them and enhance them.

you know, our customers really just need a lot of support of, like, how to scale quality content from strategy to all that goes into, like, how do you continue to adapt that over time through scaling content?
So, is that based off some of the things you're trying to assess and what your needs are, it sounds like you already have some support there and maybe that wasn't...
Is trigger for the conversation or am I maybe misinterpreting?
Yeah, we have, and we are always trying some interesting angles and perspectives because, you ours is a very, very technical product in a very technical space.
I'd love to understand what are the technical other customers you have that are very technical in nature, right?
Because I saw some of your customers, they're putting a straightforward ramp, Reddit.
I'm sure they have their own challenges, but our business is so technical because we are selling to a very technical buyer.
Yeah, it's a very technical space and we are also trying to change the landscape.
So that's what I wanted to understand.
do you guys, and have you guys operated in that space?
Do you have any expertise?
I just came across GrowthX Labs and I was reading some article on something on the VC side or maybe a CDZ or something.
And I said, you know, might as well chat or figure out if you guys are doing anything different.
Yeah, yeah.
No, I appreciate the context.
Yeah, mean, honestly, I would say our customers skew more technical than.
Not.
There are times, certainly, some of them don't, but some examples would be those like a Zooplo, which is more in the API management space.
And so for content that we're putting out for them, we're speaking particularly to developers.
who's that?
Zooplo, Z-U-P-L-O.
Zooplo, okay.
What do they do?
So they're in the API management space.
Here, I'll fill up their website so I can share.
Can I pull up my website?
Okay, got it.

Yeah, and so what we're helping them out with is both scaling up their blog content, but also their AI search strategy, because they're finding that they're actually getting a good amount of referrals and deals from AI search specifically.
So that's a big focus of what we're doing for them.
But a part of what we do for them is like breaking down, you know, how to work with different APIs.
And so we have basically built these custom workflows that...
Essentially, are taking a variety of different bits of information together.
Part of that is how the important gotchas to think about when working with varying APIs.
We're also generating code that sits within the articles and varying places that work as examples.
So that's one example of many that we work with.
Another that is probably not like necessarily as it really got, I guess, kind of depends on how you're thinking about technical.
But I think almost accidentally closed the Google Meet.
This is one of the downsides of Google Meet sometimes.
Give me one second.
trying to pull this up.
Why is this not working?
Here we go.
Sorry.
Give me just a moment.
I've got to move to Zoom.
The Google Meet is just too frustrating recently.
So here's another one.
I'm not sure if you're familiar with BAPI, but they are in the AI voice space.
This is one where essentially we have developed a set of different use case pages for them.
And so this is an example of something that's not blog content, but I do think highlights how we think and work.
In this case, you know, with our customers, we go through what we call a strategy sprint, really lay the foundation of strategy and figure out how we can go super, super fast.
There's a lot of important coordination and learning that goes into that phase.
And as we did that with the BAPI, we saw a unique opportunity to start to bring their product front and center and go after varying use cases associated to that.
And what's interesting about the product is we can actually literally like develop all these different agents around these use cases.
In this case, this page is about managing cancellations.
And specifically what we did is our workflow, I mean, we created this entire page, but our workflow.
We created the system prompt, and we basically built all these different config files, essentially, that enabled us to create these different agents, and you can literally work with this live.
Hello there.
You've reached Candy Care, where we make cancellations.
So I won't go through it now, but you can literally talk back and forth.

And so this is very specific to a Mappy, but when you find these type of opportunities, and it's going to be different for each customer of what that actually means, these can be very fruitful content opportunities to tackle.
There's a variety of other content that's in this, but not necessarily probably in the same vein as how you think about technical content, but that's another one that comes to mind.
Another big company of ours that's in the technical content space is Abnormal Security, or now as they call themselves Abnormal AI.
They are more focused on email security, have a very established content team, and quite a bit of content, so we're helping them build a variety of different sections of their website.
thing.
We see a ton here.
We're helping them enrich and build out their glossary more.
There's a ton of blog opportunities we're also working on, as well as repurposing a lot of their video content.
And so, you know, in this case, speaking to professionals, there's typically a very high bar, and we need to be quite careful with what we say.
Yeah, please, I see your hands up.
Yeah, so I think this is all helpful, Kyle.
Well, have you looked at our website?
Have you taken a look at STM?
I did yesterday, but, you know, it's transparently not as in-depth as I usually would.
This has been a day that has not gone as a plan and been pulled into a lot of different things.

No, I think, look, we're already, as I said, we are using a global provider for the content, and we're doing a lot of optimization around it, and we've been quite, we have increased the velocity of our content publishing as well.
Using a lot of modalities and channels.
I think the question becomes is, and I saw some, your pricing, which is, you know, for us, frankly, on very much on the higher side.
So the question becomes is, what's the ROI and what's the value we're going to get out of your service?
So I think it'd be good for you to maybe come back with, and just look at our website and come back with your suggestions and see if there is merit in us taking the conversation forward.
And then you can continue the conversation with John.
John, because, I mean, so far right now, what I've seen is most of the stuff we already do, and we do it fairly efficiently.
So the question becomes is, why growthx labs, right?
And what is the additional value we're going to get out of it, right?
So I think that's really what it boils down to.
Yeah, helpful context.
Happy to explore that.
Just to make sure, you know, it would be a good piece of everyone's time.
It seems like there was perhaps lanes that you did not have as much coverage that may have been top of mind.
mind for you as you're thinking about other solution providers.
I'm curious, is there anything, you know.
So that isn't as directly in the form of content that we talked about today that you were hopeful that we provide support in some means.
No, I just wanted to understand what are your breadth of services, which I think I have gotten in a sense of.
And look, we ourselves are fairly advanced in terms of our capability for CD and we are heading towards a CDZ.
So we wanted to continue to build the content flywheel because we're taking a positioning of a leader in a particular segment in the marketplace.
Right.
I think we have a good flywheel running right now.
But since I read about you guys and came across, I thought, let me see if there is something interesting that can also amplify what we are doing and continue to build on top of that.
Of course, like you know, a lot of startups, we have to be efficient from a capital allocation perspective.

So maybe the next step would be, Kyle and Bruno, is if you guys can come back with, you know, after analyzing our website and our blogs and everything and give us your view of where the GrowthX Labs can specifically help us.
I mean, that could be a very pointed.
Because like you said, Kyle, you we don't want to waste your time and our time because we're all trying to grow as fast as we can.
And congratulations on your Series A, by the way.
So I would like to keep it very efficient because if it's not a fit, that's fine.
Maybe down the line, we guys can engage you and we can stay in touch.
If there is something, then John will take it forward and we figure out a way to work with each other.
Yeah, totally.
A quick question is we're kind of like thinking about that just to make sure I'm connecting the dots properly because this could just help calibrate me.
I'm like how I think about, you know, can spork us, you know, there's typically how I think about this.
I'm just pulling up some rush.
You know, this is if you're not familiar, just don't want to assume.
But, you know, in this case, just looking at it, like it sounds like the way you're feeling good about like the volume of content you're putting out, perhaps even the quality of content.
Maybe this number is incorrect.
I guess this is somewhat of an upward trajectory, but it looks like you all haven't necessarily what I would call a lot of traction yet.
Usually what I would say...
Yeah, I think they're not happy about our authority score, for example, but it's trending in the right direction.
See, we really invested in marketing only about, honestly, four or five months ago.
So that's why you see the upward trend from October, right?
But really, the trend, really, we started focusing on was somewhere around January to February is when we started to push really hard.
And we started to sort of work on it, but there's a long way to go, and that's why we're trying to see how can we improve it further, right?
And how can we make a big difference?
Now, but the other thing we also feel is, one is SEO, but the world's really moving to GEO, right?
And a bit of optimization.
So we are also looking at some engines there as well.
Got it.
Got it.
Bernard?
Yeah, quick question there, Arshad.
What ROI are you currently getting from the program you're running versus what ROI were you expecting to get?
Because there's got to be a reason where you're still looking at options, right?
Well, I think John would be the best person to answer that question.
He has all the metrics, but I think the ROI is pretty low right now, but I think that's what we're trying to improve.
And that's why we revamped our website in the month of April, and we're going to again revamp the website next month as well.
So there's a concerted effort now going after that.
And John's our new chief marketing officer.
He joined us about two months ago.
So a lot of renewed efforts in that direction.
That's awesome.
Yeah, I think we should definitely connect with John.
Yeah, I'm certainly open to that.

John, you know, can share a little bit more of like how we approach building strategy with the platform in the background, et cetera, from an ROI perspective, not surprising to hear, you know, not seeing amazing ROI.

That's one of the interesting challenges when it comes to organic, and a lot of times going back to my journey when I was hired at varying stops is, you know, it's kind of like thought about but pushed to the side because the quick ones can come from paid.
Because usually when you look at it from a, you know, a, say, three-month horizon, maybe even six months, depending on your space and strategy, you know, paid is going to be a better ROI.
But where the game really starts to change is 12, 18 months plus, and where it's not even comparable, right?
And it starts to compound.
And so ultimately, like, when we think about who are the ideal fits for us, it's those that are trying to build to that point.

The ones that tend to not be the most ideal for us, especially those that are, like, early on their content journey, understanding that there's the pressures of, like, you know, building your go-to-market motion early on and getting that momentum.
You know, if you need revenue impact by month three, that's typically not going to be where, like, the type of work that we do is, it's not that it's not going to show progress and signal, but the
Going back to some of those ROI conversations, that can be an important point of alignment.
Usually my take in consultation around that is just, yeah, that might just be a signal that it's not the right time to invest in this and other channels, right?
I'm sure if applicable here, but just, you know, given some of the things you shared, just want to note that.

Yeah, so I think maybe if you guys are okay, you guys can analyze us outside in, give us your perspective where you can help us and be very pointed around these elements like authority score, traffic, return on investment, you know, keywords that we are showing up on, et cetera, because we're also launching a big agentic layer on top of our platform in the next couple of months.
That's going to get a lot of visibility as well.
So we really want to amplify and double down on our marketing and content marketing is one piece of that.
So as I found you guys, I set it up directly myself and, you know, John was unfortunately traveling, but
We could come back and have a take a look at it.
Yeah, no, sounds good.
I'm curious as we go through that, are there those that are either you consider like direct competitors or at least tertiary?
I think our direct competitors are companies like Spacelift, Harness, Control Monkey.
You know, those are some of the very technical products and practical spaces.
Increasingly, our AI agentically are coming in.
I think we'll be competing against Traversal and Resolve.ai.
So I think those are the two additional players that are there on the right.
We are on the left, but we're expanding horizontally.
Super helpful.
Yeah, certainly happy to dig in a bit more there.
gives us a little bit of inspiration context.
We'll follow up with the recap.
You know, feel free to share this recording with John as well.
And, you know, we'll make sure he's on the email, but, you know, any questions that come up in the meantime, you know, happy to get a follow up going sometime probably next.
week and see if there's a fit.
OK.
Thank you, guys.
Thank you very much.
Appreciate it.
Take care.
Thank you.
Pleasure meeting.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between StackGen and GrowthX to explore potential collaboration on content marketing and growth strategies.](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [StackGen operates in a highly technical B2B dev tooling space, seeking to improve content marketing ROI](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=484.0)
  - [GrowthX specializes in building organic content engines, with a focus on technical content for B2B SaaS companies](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=264.0)
  - [StackGen has existing content production but is open to exploring ways to amplify their efforts and improve metrics](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=826.0)
  - [Next steps involve GrowthX analyzing StackGen's website and providing specific recommendations for improvement](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=854.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Expertise and Approach

  - [Founded by Marcel (CEO), known for Arian Gara workflows for fast, high-quality content production](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
  - [Recently closed Series A funding, now at 70 employees](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
  - [Specializes in technical content creation, particularly for B2B SaaS and DevTool companies](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [Uses AI-driven workflows and a team of experts to scale content production](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Focuses on editorial content first, then expands to other areas (newsletters, lead magnets, social)](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=264.0)
  - [Aims to help clients think and act like publishers, creating highly valuable content for specific personas](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)

### StackGen's Current Marketing Efforts

  - [Using a third-party service provider for content production](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
  - [Increased content publishing velocity in recent months](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
  - [Invested heavily in marketing starting \~4-5 months ago (Jan-Feb 2025)](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1047.0)
  - [Current ROI on content marketing efforts is low, seeking improvement](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)
  - [Planning website revamps to enhance performance](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1101.0)
  - [New CMO (John) joined \~2 months ago, leading renewed marketing efforts](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1111.0)

### Technical Content Examples from GrowthX

  - [Zuplo (API management): Blog content and AI search strategy, including code examples](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [BAPI (AI voice): Use case pages with interactive AI agents](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Abnormal Security/AI (email security): Glossary enrichment, blog content, video repurposing](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=768.0)

### StackGen's Competitive Landscape

  - [Direct competitors: Spacelift, Harness, Control Monkey](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1272.0)
  - [Emerging AI-focused competitors: Traversal, Resolve.ai](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1283.0)
  - [StackGen positioning as a leader in a specific market segment](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1248.0)
  - [Upcoming launch of a major "agentic layer" on their platform](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1246.0)

### Content Performance Metrics

  - [StackGen's authority score is improving but not satisfactory](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1031.0)
  - [Upward trend in content metrics since October 2024, with significant push from January 2025](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1043.0)
  - [Exploring both SEO and AI-driven search optimization (GEO)](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1063.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to analyze StackGen's website, blog, and content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1298.0)
  - [Provide specific recommendations focusing on:](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1298.0)
      - [Improving authority score](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1031.0)
      - [Increasing traffic](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1022.0)
      - [Enhancing return on investment](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)
      - [Targeting valuable keywords](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1220.0)
  - [Schedule a follow-up meeting next week with John (StackGen's CMO) to discuss findings and potential collaboration](https://fathom.video/share/36g4UxJAUKxzxsVFU548YRtYSoeUFz4W?tab=summary&timestamp=1311.0)

action_items.description: Analyze StackGen website/blogs. Provide specific suggestions on how GrowthX Labs can help. Focus on authority score, traffic, ROI, keywords. Consider upcoming agentic layer launch. Include competitors (Spacelift, Harness, Control Monkey, Traversal, Resolve.ai). Send recap email w/ John cc'd. Propose follow-up meeting next week.
Collaborate w/ Kyle on StackGen website analysis. Contribute to specific suggestions for GrowthX Labs assistance. Help prepare recap email and follow-up meeting proposal.
